sources.label: 2023-04-30 special foundations Q&A 3min vs 30min ('we are never late')

segments.label: Cold open: the Ranger point man
segments.status: MINED (verified vs VTT; trim in cutroom)
segments.quote_anchor: you don't put a guy walking point who doesn't want to be there... that feeling of responsibility and presence and mindfulness, confident in the knowledge that i was willing trained fit and prepared... equals ready to act

alternates.label: full point-man story w/ platoon diagrams

segments.why: His zero-state origin story with combat stakes, landing on 'ready to act' — the phrase that recurs all episode.

segments.label: What it is, in his words (canonical definition)
segments.status: MINED (verified vs VTT; consider trimming the grid aside ~140-175s in cutroom)
segments.quote_anchor: the zero state — a self-defined state of awareness and readiness which is your optimal trading state... ready — ready for what — ready to act
segments.why: The definition delivered to camera at the top of the segment. The 'muscle' alternate pairs well appended.

segments.label: EXPLAINER: the practice card (typography only)
segments.vo_script: DRAFT (every step traces to transcript passages — see practice_card_trace): His reset, in his own words. Take a deep breath. Write down everything that is making you mad, every grievance, on one card. Flip it over. Name each feeling in one word. Stay with them until the inside is empty. Seal the cards. Burn them, and watch the smoke until it is gone. Then do the next obvious thing. And when there is no time for the ritual, the speed run: one clap, one deep breath. Let's go. The old samurai manual says within seven breaths the warrior is ready to commit. He asks his traders: how many breaths does it take you?
segments.animation_brief: NEW COMPONENT: typography-only practice card. No charts. Animated checklist card, one line landing at a time with the VO, generous dwell. Dark theme, owl-amber accent, large type.

practice_card_trace.owl_meditation_steps: strat2328 2429-2706s: deep breath; grievances on a 3x5/5x8 card; flip, name each emotion one word; until inside is empty; envelope, stone bowl, burn, watch smoke dissipate
practice_card_trace.speed_run: strat2328 2671-2706s: 'a clap on my hand and one deep breath — let's go, bang, i'm in the zero state'
practice_card_trace.seven_breaths: swinglesson 1908-1925s: 'the hagakure says within seven breaths the samurai is ready to commit'; 'how many heartbeats does it take you' 1867-1925s

segments.label: Him coaching it live: George's exits
segments.status: MINED (verified vs VTT; starts at 'my coaching with George' 3192s to skip prior whiteboard reference; full context from 3174s)
segments.quote_anchor: he makes his entries he is in the zero state, but when he makes his exits he's not... your emotional state is always in the professional zero state band so that you can trade many decisions no matter which way it's going and simply respond to price

alternates.label: 'we are never late' Q&A reset (tight 90s)

alternates.label: 'how many heartbeats' challenge to the room

segments.why: Named real coaching relationship, specific diagnosis (entries yes, exits no), and a prescription. Ends on the cohort call-and-response.

segments.label: When it fails: his own lapse (Rivian)
segments.quote_anchor: got me out at just a minus two R loss... but i didn't have the willpower or the zero state to turn that into a stop and reverse... there was clearly too much emotion
